report.summary: Standup v10 Apr 3: BTC holds ~$66,500 (flat from v9), ETH ~$2,055. F&G at 9 — Extreme Fear day 8+. Since v9: publish agent promoted 2 pages (2 deferred), eod-v9 flagged defi-yields API missing cache/fallback as critical. 33 agent runs today = ~$2,238 human-equiv. Git rebase conflict blocking deploy — 19th deploy-debt escalation.

sections.title: Analytics
items: DELTA SINCE LAST STANDUP (v9 → v10): BTC flat at ~$66,500, no meaningful move from v9's $66,503. ETH unchanged at ~$2,055. Market in low-vol consolidation near $66.5K support. Total crypto market cap $2.38T.

F&G INDEX: Holds at 9 — Extreme Fear. Unchanged from v9. Sub-10 territory for 8+ consecutive days. April 1 reading was 8, slight recovery to 9. Historically rare sustained extreme fear — last comparable streak was June 2022 (Terra-Luna collapse, bottomed at 6).

BTC ETF FLOWS: April 1 saw $173.7M net outflows (IBIT -$86.5M, FBTC -$78.6M). Q1 2026 closed at ~$500M net outflows overall, but March's $1.32B inflows showed institutional resilience. April 2-3 specific flow data not yet available — monitoring for stabilization signals.

MORGAN STANLEY MSBT ETF: Still the top content opportunity. S-1 Amendment No. 4 filed April 1 with 0.14% annual fee — lowest-cost BTC ETF, undercutting IBIT (0.25%). No new developments since v9 but the first-mover SEO window is narrowing.
STABLECOIN SUPPLY: $316B ATH — record dry powder sitting on sidelines during extreme fear. Historically bullish divergence signal when combined with sub-10 F&G.

CONTENT ON DISK: 311 learn page dirs (cryptodegen) + 78 page.tsx (Degen0x). Publish agent promoted 2 pages this cycle (Drift Protocol hack analysis, Polkadot staking halving guide). 2 remain in PUBLISH_QUEUE (impermanent loss, Hyperliquid — both missing page.tsx).

sections.title: Project Manager
items: SINCE v9: 3 agent runs — publish (11:21 UTC, promoted 2 pages), eod-v9 (11:55 UTC, QA cycle-close), standup v10 (this cycle). No build, design, or community agents this cycle.
TODAY'S FULL AGENT ACTIVITY: 33 runs across 6 types — standup×10 ($450), build×3 ($699), design×3 ($180), community×7 ($287), eod×9 ($567), publish×1 ($55). Total human-equiv: ~$2,238.

P0 — GIT REBASE CONFLICT (NEW): Publish agent discovered git rebase in progress on main branch. Deploy is blocked until this is manually resolved. This compounds the deploy debt — now 19th consecutive escalation.

P0 — DEPLOY DEBT (19TH ESCALATION): Same as v9 but worse — rebase conflict adds a new blocker on top of the existing uncommitted content. 311+ learn pages invisible to Google. JEFE MUST resolve rebase + push.

P0 — DEFI-YIELDS API CRITICAL: eod-v9 flagged that /api/defi-yields has NO in-memory cache and NO fallback data. If DefiLlama goes down, users see raw errors. All other 5 API routes have proper caching.
P1 — 108 PHANTOM PUBLISHED SLUGS: 108 slugs in published-pages.ts with no page.tsx on disk. Will 404 if deployed. Unchanged 19+ cycles.
P1 — 2 PAGES STUCK IN QUEUE: impermanent-loss and Hyperliquid guides deferred — both missing page.tsx. Need scaffold before next publish cycle.
P2 — TS ERRORS: 15 in structured-data.examples.ts (needs .tsx rename). Non-blocking due to ignoreBuildErrors.

sections.title: CEO — Next Cycle Priorities
items: PRIORITY 1 — RESOLVE REBASE + DEPLOY (P0 CRITICAL — 19TH ESCALATION): A git rebase conflict is now actively blocking deployment. This is the #1 blocker. Steps: (1) resolve rebase on main, (2) commit all content, (3) push to trigger Vercel deploy. Every hour of delay = permanent organic traffic loss during peak extreme-fear search demand.

PRIORITY 2 — FIX DEFI-YIELDS API: The stablecoin yield tracker tool shipped but its API route has no cache or fallback. One DefiLlama outage = broken tool for users. Add LRU cache + TTL + stale fallback matching the pattern used in prices/trending/market-dominance routes. Quick fix, high impact.

PRIORITY 3 — SCAFFOLD 2 MISSING PAGES + MSBT CONTENT: (a) Build page.tsx for impermanent-loss and Hyperliquid guides so they can clear the publish queue. (b) Create Morgan Stanley MSBT ETF content — first-mover SEO window closing. Target: 'Morgan Stanley Bitcoin ETF', 'MSBT ETF', 'cheapest Bitcoin ETF' queries.

report.email: Resend API blocked by browser CORS (TypeError: Failed to fetch). Consistent with v1-v9. Report saved to reports/ folder.
